steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The subject is "collection" (singular). "of oil paintings" is a prepositional phrase modifying "collection," and "which includes James Ensor's Tribulations... and Yasuo Kuniyoshi's Self-Portrait..." is a nonrestrictive relative clause — neither changes the subject's number.

steps.title: Rule: singular subject requires singular verb
steps.content:
Per-choice diagnosis:

A. "remain" — plural verb (used with they); disagrees with singular "collection." Incorrect.

B. "have remained" — plural present-perfect verb; disagrees with singular "collection." Incorrect.

C. "remains" — singular present-tense verb. The collection remains — subject and verb agree. Correct.

D. "have been remaining" — plural progressive perfect; disagrees with singular "collection" and is awkward. Incorrect.

C is correct.
